April 8, ]-99]-

Kurt Hahn
Finance Director
City Ha I l--Administrative Offices
P. O. Box 578
125 Matheson street
Heal,dsburg, CA 95444-057I
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. l-91-o37
Dear I'1r . Hahn :
You have requested advice concerning the application of the
conf l ict-of- inteiest provisions of the Pol'itical Reform Act (the
rra"1rr1.1 Because youi questions do not pertain to a specific
pending decision, we are treating your request as one for informal
aavice.2
auEEr-I-9!S
1. 
You are considering investing city funds in a local
bank. one of your city counciLnembers is a rnaior investor and
director of the bank. Does the councilmember's investment in and
position with the bank preclude you from investing the city's
rnoney in the bank or accepting security from the bank?
2. 
would the counc i Imernber' s participation in city action
concerning the bank preclude you from taking actions on city
business with the bank?
3. 
Does the city councilmember's investment in and position
with the bank preclude the city froro engaging in other business
retationships with the bank?
1 Go,r"..,."nt Code Sections 81OOO-91-015. ALL statutory references
are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated' 
Comnission
reguLations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations . Section
18500, et seq-.- A1l, references to regulations are to Title 2'
oivision O of the cal-ifornia Code of Regulations'
provide the requestor with the
f ormal- written advice. (Section
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O? . Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 322'5660


our File No. I -91-0 3 7
Page 2
4. 
Do we have any guidelines to avoid even the appearance
of a conflict of interest?
CONCLUSION
1. 
The councilmernber' s investment in and position with the
bank does not cause a conflict of interest that precludes you from
doing business lrith the bank, investing the city's rnoney in the
bank, or accepting security fron the bank.
2- 
The councilnember's participation in city action would
not precl,ude you from takinq actions on city business with the
bank .
3. 
The city councilmember's investment in and position with
the bank does not preclude the city from engaging in other banking
relationships with the bank.
4. 
Any additional action to elininate any further
appearance oi a conflict of interest is left to you, the city and
the councilrnember.
FACTS
You are the finance director/ assistant treasurer for the city
of Healdsburg. In that capacity you are responsible for
investigating and managing the city's idl-e cash and investment
portfolio. 
Recentty you were approached by the president of
ivi.,d=or oaks Nationlf eank (the irbankr') to invest city funds in
bank certificates of deposit. one of your city councilmembers,
Edgar Deas, is a major investor in and director of the bank.
The city's depository resolution requires, dependent on the
particular tiansaction, two or four signatures, irc-:, the.city
ir.n.ger, finance dj-rector, mayor or vice rnayor. on occasion, but
not typically, the mayor or vice mayor rnay sign such documents
tnus lpproving such a transaction. Mr. Deas is the vice mayor
this year and would becone the mayor next year. According to you,
the signing of such docurnents is ministerial in nature.
rnasmuch as the bank includes a number of area developers
among its stockholders and on its board of directors, it is 1ikely
that the bank will be the provider of various kinds of security
for city development projects. 
Normally, the finance director
approves or disapproves such security.
You fear that any direct banking relationship with the bank
creates the appearance of conflict and therefore should be
avoided. on tire other hand the city is assertively working with
al-l- banks in the area to focus theii efforts under the Cornrnunity
Reinvestment Act.


Our File No. 
■-9■-037
Page 3
ANALYSI S
L, 
Does the councilrnember's investment in and posi,tion with
Section 8710o prohibits any public official 
from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his official 
position
to inf l-uence a governmental, decision in which the official 
has a
financiaf interest.
The Act provides a four-part test to deterrnine lrhether a
public official 
has a conflict of interest in a governnental
decision. First, is the officiat considering rnaking, participat-
ing in rnaking, or using his officia), position to influence a
qovernmental decision? (Section 87100.) Second, is it
ioreseeable that the decision will affect the official's 
economic
interests? 
(Section 87103.) Third, is the effect of the decision
on the officiaL's 
economic interests material? (Id.) 
Fourth, is
the effect of the decision on the officiaf's 
economic interests
distinquishable fron its effect on the public qenerallv? (Id.)
Each oi these elenents must be ruet before there is a conflict of
interest. 
Here we need to go no further than the second question
to deternine that you do not have a conflict of interest.
A. 
Makinq or Participating In A Governmental Decision
An off icj.a1 makes a governnental decision when he votes, com-
rnits his agency to a course of action, enters into a contract, or
appoints sorneone. (Regul-ation 18700(b), coPY encl,osed.) Deciding
whether to invest city funds with the bank or deciding $rhether to
accept the bank's security are governmental decisions.
B. 
Foreseeable Financial Effect on An Economic Tnterest
The second issue is the foreseeability that the decision will
affect ygU! economic interests. 
The paraneters of a public
official-'s 
econorni-c interests are set forth in Section 87103-
An official 
has a financial interest i.n a
deci,sion within the meaning of section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision wiIl,
have a material financial effect, 
on the
officiat or a member of his or her inmediate fanily
or on:(a) Any business entity in which the public
official- has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,00o) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars (51,o00) or more.


our File No. I -91-o 3 7
Page 4
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a comnercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public wi-thout regard to
officiat 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars (S25o) or nore in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time $rhen the
decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
j,s a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of managernent.
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
tr^/o hundred fifty dollars ($25o) or more in value
prov.ided to, received by, or prornised to the public
official 
within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
Section 87103 (a) - (e) .
In your case, there wiII be no foreseeable financial- effect
on you or your economic interests. 
From the facts provided, Mr.
Deas may have an economic interest in the decision and may have a
conflict of interest if he participates in the decision to invest
in the bank.3 Mr. Deas's economic interests, however, will not
3 w" do not provide advice to third parties about another
person's duties or obligations unless the third party has been
authorized to request advice by the person whose duties or
obligations are in question. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329,
copy enclosed. ) Since l'1r. Deas has not authorized you to request
advice on his behalf, we cannot provide advice about his
obtigations. 
P1ease note however that a public official 
who makes
or participates in a governnental decision or otherwise uses his
official 
influence where he has a conflict of interest rnay be
subject to the civil and crirninal penalties of the Act, and the
decision may be declared void. 
(Sections 91ooo, et seq. ) Making
or participating in making a governmental decision is defined in
Regulation 18700 (copy enclosed) . Using one's official position
to influence a decision is defined in Regulation 18700.1 (copy
enclosed) .
As discussed above, generally speaking, a conflict of
interest may arise if a public official,s 
decision would have a
reasonably foreseeable and material financial effect on a business
entity in which the public offj-cial has a direct or indirect
investment of $1,000 or more or in which the public official 
is a
director, officer partner, trustee, employee, or holds any
position of management. (Section 87103 (a) , (d) . ) For your
information, f have enclosed a copy of Regulation 1,8202.2 that
addresses when a financiaf effect upon a business entity is
material and may cause a conflict of interest.


our Fife
Page 5
cause a
economic
funds in
No. I -91-0 3 7
conflict of interest in your decisions. Thus, Mr. Deas's
interests will not preclude you from deciding to invest
the bank or accept the bank's security.

not cause a conflict of interest for you。
  (Section 87■03.)
relationshios With the bank?
As discussed above, the Act prohibits publ-ig.-e!!&ia-I-E. f ron
taking official 
actions r./hen they have a conffict of interest'
(See Section 87100.) It does not create an absolute ban on
sovernmental entitj.es where one of their officials 
may have a
Eo.,tf ict oe i.nt"r"st. 
Thus, Mr. Deas's economic interest in a
particular governmentaL decision alone wiII not preclude others in
Lhe city from rnaking decisions concerning the bank.
4. 
Do we have anY quidelj,nes to avoid even the aDpearance
of a conftict of interest?
The conf lict-of- interest provisions of the Act are themselves
designed to prevent conf l j.cts of interest and the appearance of
any 6onf1ict. 
Your city may choose on.its orrln to avoid a
reiationship with the blnk if the prohibitions of the Act do not
elirninate the appearance of a 6onflict to your satisfaction'
Alternativefy, lai. Deas may choose to retinquish his investment
and position-with the bank. Those decisions, however, are left to
you, the citY and I{r- Deas.
n 
Af=o note that ministerial actions do not constitute the
maxint-"r farticipating in naking o{ a governmental decision and
thus iould not cause a conflict 5f interest' 
(Regulation
iiiiooiaf , "opy enclosed-) For example, as long as the-signer of
ah" ;li;;k ha's- no discretion, the act is ministeriat and would not
t;ig;;; 
a confLict of interest for the signer'. (t:"-*Xlt*.lu"i""
Letter, No. A-84-152, (copy enclosed) ' ) ie make no determination
here as to whether l,1.. 'D"ii'. actions are indeed mi'nisterial'

matter ,

.
